what_it_does: Defines a standalone HTML probe page that mocks corpus and neighbour fetch responses, binds a projection drill into a gallery mount, dispatches a projection:select event, and displays a status message when gallery:rendered fires.
observations: Loads shared piece and phone styles plus organisms.js and unit-view.js.
Defines inline probe styling for the page caption and flow status element.
Stubs window.fetch to return a corpus record or neighbour list based on the request URL.
Uses a capture record for src/distill/harness.ts under the common_knowledge projection.
Provides five neighbour source addresses with numeric scores.
Listens for gallery:rendered and writes the rendered source into #flow.
Dispatches projection:select immediately after binding the projection drill.
standouts: The page exercises an event-driven render path using mocked same-page data.
The mock data includes both a corpus record and a neighbour constellation.
The visible status text tracks the probe flow from waiting state to gallery-rendered event.
conventions: Uses inline script and inline CSS appropriate to a probe fixture.
Uses global browser events as the coordination mechanism.
Uses code:// and run:// address strings as data identifiers.
Overrides window.fetch directly for test isolation inside the page.
concerns: The global fetch override is broad and returns the corpus response for any non-neighbour URL.
The mocked fetch responses only implement the fields used by the page and may not behave like full Response objects.
The page dispatches projection:select immediately, so it assumes the imported scripts have synchronously registered the needed APIs.
notes: A browser-level probe for the projection-to-gallery live path with mocked corpus and neighbour data.
questions: What exact request URL shape is expected for the corpus fetch?
What detail payload is expected from gallery:rendered beyond source?
Is the broad fallback corpus response intentional for every non-neighbour fetch?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it probes projection selection through mocked corpus and live-neighbour-shaped responses into a gallery render event.
intention_signals: It seems intended to verify that selecting a corpus projection can drive gallery rendering while preserving the neighbour constellation wiring shape expected by the live path.
novelty: A self-contained HTML harness uses synthetic run:// and code:// addresses with event dispatch and mocked HTTP-like responses to exercise a UI integration path.
contribution: Contributes a browser probe that preserves the projection selection event flow, mocked corpus and neighbour response shapes, and gallery-rendered confirmation path for a fused system.
summary_for_embedding: Standalone HTML probe that mocks corpus and neighbour fetches, binds DNA projection drill rendering, dispatches projection:select, and records gallery:rendered status.
intention_for_embedding: It was for checking the projection-driven gallery render path using realistic record and neighbour payload shapes without requiring real network services.
